channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: NFCShare Android malware spreads via fake banking app updates on GitHub
item.description: New variants of the NFCShare Android malware are being distributed as fake updates for legitimate banking apps hosted on GitHub. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SoFi confirms third-party data breach at Hong Kong subsidiary
item.description: SoFi Hong Kong is warning that it suffered a data breach after hackers gained access to a database at a third-party vendor containing customer information. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Apple feature automatically changes your compromised passwords
item.description: At WWDC 26, Apple announced an Apple Intelligence-powered feature that can automatically fix weak and compromised passwords. This works in Safari, and it's rolling out with iOS 27. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
item.description: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Shai-Hulud attack trojanizes 19 science-focused PyPI packages
item.description: Hackers compromised 19 packages on the PyPI, collectively downloaded hundreds of thousands of times, in a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain attack that delivered malware designed to steal developer secrets....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
item.description: A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: One-Character Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Local Root Access, Exploits Now Public
item.description:
Security researchers have published a detailed, working exploit for a Linux kernel use-after-free that lets an unprivileged local user escalate to root and break out of a container.
The flaw, CVE-202...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
item.description: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: WhatsApp says it disrupted new NSO spyware phishing attacks
item.description: WhatsApp has detected and stopped spear-phishing campaigns allegedly conducted by the NSO Group after investigating user reports of social engineering attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Meta Blocks NSO Group's New WhatsApp Phishing Attack, Files Contempt Order
item.description:
Meta on Monday said it detected and blocked spear-phishing attempts linked to Israeli spyware vendor NSO Group.
In addition, the tech giant said it's filing a federal court contempt order against the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Gogs patches critical zero-day enabling remote code execution
item.description: Gogs has patched a critical security zero-day flaw that can allow attackers to compromise Internet-facing instances and access any repositories (including private ones). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'Hades' Campaign Against PyPI Puts New Spin on Shai-Hulud
item.description: The latest attacks, which hit 37 PyPI wheels and 19 code packages, show a continued evolution of the persistent software supply chain threat....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Critical UniFi OS bug lets hackers gain root without authentication
item.description: Attackers can chain three already fixed vulnerabilities in the Ubiquiti UniFi OS server to execute remote code with root privileges and without authentication. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited to Bypass Passwords in IKEv1 Setups
item.description: Check Point has warned of active exploitation of a critical vulnerability impacting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments that are configured to use the deprecated IKEv1 key exchange protoco...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Reducing security operations complexity with Wazuh Cloud
item.description: Security teams are increasingly overwhelmed by alert fatigue, infrastructure maintenance, and complex hybrid environments. This article explores how Wazuh Cloud helps simplify SIEM/XDR operations thro...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Phishing Is Crushing SOCs with Alert Volume: How to Reduce Tier 1 Overload
item.description:
Phishing has always been a numbers game. AI has turned it into a volume machine.
Attackers can now create convincing emails, fake login pages, and tailored lures in minutes. Every polished message ad...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Instagram Account Hacks, Android Zero-Day, GitHub Worm and More
item.description:
Monday again. The weekend was meant to be quiet. It wasn't. Last week had poisoned packages, a broken AI helper, and a worm tearing through repos. The ugly part: basic tricks still worked.
A chatbot ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Check Point links VPN zero-day attacks to Qilin ransomware gang
item.description: Israeli cybersecurity company Check Point has released security updates to patch a critical flaw affecting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments, which was exploited in zero-day attacks. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Hardest Fork
item.description: Mythos is real. I know a big chunk of the industry thinks it's a marketing stunt, and I get why. I get it. But I've seen the findings, and they're bad. These aren't "whoops, this line right here is wr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Oxford University discloses data breach after careers platform hack
item.description: The University of Oxford disclosed a new data breach last week after being informed by its third-party provider, Group GTI, that its CareerConnect career services platform had been compromised. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: VerdantBamboo Deploys BSD Variant of BRICKSTORM on Linux Appliances
item.description: A China-nexus cyber espionage group has been observed deploying a BSD variant of a known backdoor called BRICKSTORM, as well as two other malware families codenamed PLENET (aka GRIMBOLT) and AGENTPSD ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign that has targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial services i...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: VS Code Adds 2-Hour Extension Auto-Update Delay to Limit Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Microsoft has announced that Visual Studio Code (VS Code) will apply a two-hour delay before extensions for the integrated development environment (IDE) are updated automatically to a newer version in...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Over 20,000 Instagram accounts stolen in Meta AI support hack
item.description: Meta has revealed that 20,225 Instagram users had their accounts hijacked in a recent incident where attackers used Meta's AI-powered support system to reset passwords. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hands on with Intelligent Terminal, an AI-powered Windows Terminal
item.description: Microsoft has created an open-source fork of Windows Terminal called "Intelligent Terminal," and it allows you to use AI directly inside Terminal without interfering with the regular session. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: C0XMO botnet spreads via DD-WRT router flaw, kills rival malware
item.description: A new variant of the Gafgyt botnet called C0XMO is targeting DD-WRT router firmware and can move to other device types with various CPU architectures. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Silent Ransom Group targets law firms with fake IT support calls
item.description: The Silent Ransom Group extortion gang is actively targeting U.S. law firms and professional services organizations in social engineering attacks that often lead to data theft within hours of initial ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Everest Forms Pro flaw exploited to take over WordPress sites
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-3300) in the Everest Forms Pro plugin, which lets them take complete control of a WordPress website. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration
item.description:
OpenAI has begun rolling out a new Lockdown Mode to ChatGPT for eligible personal accounts to reduce the risk of data exfiltration arising from prompt injection attacks.
The feature is primarily desi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
